verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the concrete default plan, 'recurring subscriptions + Stripe-hosted Checkout'; B also starts with defaults but adds an unasked stack assumption, 'Node/TypeScript-ish backend'.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to one recommendation: 'what I'd recommend by default'; B is decisive on hosted Checkout but partially surveys stack assumptions.
verdict.fabrication: B invents a stack assumption: 'Node/TypeScript-ish backend'; A's assumptions are billing-pattern defaults, not repo-specific facts.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it is tighter and ordered as 'The plan' with exact endpoints/methods like 'stripe.checkout.sessions.create' and 'stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create'.
